projects.question_en: Does an MRI quality-control pipeline (MRIQC) calibrated on healthy controls generalize to pre- and post-surgical trigeminal neuralgia patients, or do surgical/pathological changes cause systematic domain shift in QC metrics (CJV, CNR, SNR, EFC, FBER, FWHM, tSNR)?
projects.question_es: ¿Un pipeline de control de calidad de RM (MRIQC) calibrado en controles sanos generaliza a pacientes con neuralgia del trigémino pre- y posquirúrgicos, o los cambios quirúrgicos/patológicos provocan un desplazamiento de dominio sistemático en las métricas de QC (CJV, CNR, SNR, EFC, FBER, FWHM, tSNR)?

required_variables.name: MRIQC image-quality metrics (CJV, CNR, SNR for structural; EFC, FBER, FWHM, SNR, tSNR for functional)
required_variables.evidence_quote: The outputs of the whole dataset MRIQC metrics are available in the “mriqc_data” folder (in .tsv and .html formats).

required_variables.name: group/session label distinguishing healthy controls, pre-surgical patients, and 6-month follow-up patients
required_variables.file: BIDS folder naming convention (sub-** = controls, sub-*** = patients, sub-***fu = follow-up) plus participants.tsv
required_variables.evidence_quote: structural and functional MRI volumes for 112 patients with PTN, organized in folders labeled sub-***, and 48 healthy volunteers in folders labeled sub-**... 6-month follow-up MRI data are available for 58 patients stored in folders labeled sub-***fu.

projects.compute: Laptop-level; MRIQC outputs are already precomputed and provided, so no image processing or GPU is required.
projects.still_open_because: The data descriptor (src e1) reports that MRIQC was run to validate the dataset, but only describes the metrics qualitatively (checking for artifacts like aliasing/distortion/ghosting) and never compares QC-metric distributions across the control, pre-surgical, and post-surgical follow-up groups, or tests whether thresholds derived from one group misclassify scans from another.

projects.question_en: In patients with trigeminal neuralgia, does combining structural connectivity (DTI-derived fractional anisotropy along trigeminal/pain-related white-matter tracts) with functional connectivity (resting-state fMRI) explain more variance in self-reported average pain intensity (BPI-facial) than either modality alone?
projects.question_es: En pacientes con neuralgia del trigémino, ¿combinar la conectividad estructural (anisotropía fraccional derivada de DTI en tractos de sustancia blanca relacionados con el trigémino/dolor) con la conectividad funcional (fMRI en reposo) explica más varianza en la intensidad de dolor promedio autoreportada (BPI-facial) que cualquiera de las dos modalidades por separado?

required_variables.name: Diffusion tensor imaging, 64 directions (b=1500) plus b0 runs for TOPUP distortion correction
required_variables.file: dwi folder (*_run-01.nii.gz main DTI; *_run-02/03/04.nii.gz b0 runs)
required_variables.evidence_quote: the “dwi” folder contains diffusion tensor imaging data with 64 diffusion directions (*_run-01.nii.gz file) and three b0-only runs... acquired for spatial distortion correction with the FSL TOPUP procedure

required_variables.name: Resting-state fMRI, 200 volumes (eyes closed)
required_variables.evidence_quote: the “func” folder includes functional resting-state BOLD-EPI MRI data (4D data with 200 volumes per participant, excluding dummy scans)

required_variables.name: Average pain intensity (BPI-facial, week preceding admission)
required_variables.evidence_quote: The dataset contains only data on the average pain levels of patients during the week preceding hospital admission... including the type, intensity, and side of pain

projects.compute: Workstation with FSL/MRtrix3 installed, ~50-100GB local storage for preprocessed derivatives; no GPU strictly required but speeds up fMRIPrep.
projects.still_open_because: The data descriptor (src e1) explicitly separates the two modalities in its own follow-up work (DTI-only tract-based spatial statistics [ref CR9] and thalamic volumetry [ref CR11] are cited as separate prior papers by the same team), but nothing in the evidence shows a joint structural+functional model against the BPI-facial pain score being reported; the descriptor itself invites this by noting the dataset lets researchers 'independently define the focus of their specific research interests.'

projects.question_en: Using the paired pre-surgical and ~6-month post-surgical MRI available for 58 patients, do longitudinal within-subject changes in structural MRI (e.g., FreeSurfer-derived subcortical/thalamic volumes from the T1w/T2w scans) track with surgical outcome (favorable vs. unfavorable pain reduction), pointing to a treatment-response correlate rather than a static pre-surgical biomarker?
projects.question_es: Usando la RM pareada pre- y ~6 meses posquirúrgica disponible para 58 pacientes, ¿los cambios longitudinales intrasujeto en la RM estructural (p. ej., volúmenes subcorticales/talámicos derivados con FreeSurfer a partir de las secuencias T1w/T2w) se relacionan con el desenlace quirúrgico (reducción de dolor favorable vs. desfavorable), señalando un correlato de respuesta al tratamiento en lugar de un biomarcador estático prequirúrgico?

required_variables.name: Paired baseline and 6-month follow-up defaced T1w/T2w structural scans for 58 patients
required_variables.file: anat folder in sub-*** (baseline) and sub-***fu (follow-up) directories
required_variables.evidence_quote: the “anat” folder contains high-resolution defaced structural images (T1w and T2w) suitable for automatic segmentation with FreeSurfer... Furthermore, 6-month follow-up MRI data are available for 58 patients stored in folders labeled sub-***fu... (mean follow-up time = 214.8 days, std = 43.3)

required_variables.name: Surgical outcome classification (favorable vs. unfavorable, based on % pain reduction) and surgical procedure type
required_variables.evidence_quote: The surgical outcome was classified as favorable or unfavorable on the basis of the extent of pain reduction following the procedure... surgical procedure type are provided in the “participants.tsv” file.

projects.compute: Workstation or small cluster access for FreeSurfer recon-all on ~116 T1w/T2w volumes (58 patients x 2 timepoints); each run is CPU-bound, ~6-12h per subject, parallelizable.
projects.still_open_because: The paper's own cited follow-up work (thalamic nuclei volumetry, ref CR11) reports 'no significant increase in volume in any of the examined nuclei six months after successful surgical intervention' for a subcortical ROI, but src e1 gives no evidence that a whole-brain longitudinal change-vs-outcome model has been run on the full dataset; the descriptor explicitly lists pre/post comparison stratified by surgical outcome as an open direction ('comparative analysis of pre- and postoperative changes... depending on the surgical outcome').

projects.question_en: Does an MRI-based classifier of favorable vs. unfavorable surgical outcome perform equally well for patients with clear neurovascular compression (Sindou-graded on MRI) as for idiopathic patients with no identified compression, and does classifier performance differ by sex (56% female in the surgical group vs. 89% female in the conservatively-managed outpatient subgroup)?
projects.question_es: ¿Un clasificador basado en RM del desenlace quirúrgico favorable vs. desfavorable rinde igual de bien en pacientes con compresión neurovascular clara (graduada según Sindou en la RM) que en pacientes idiopáticos sin compresión identificada, y el rendimiento del clasificador difiere según el sexo (56% mujeres en el grupo quirúrgico vs. 89% mujeres en el subgrupo ambulatorio de manejo conservador)?

required_variables.name: Neurovascular compression grade (Sindou classification, when applicable)
required_variables.evidence_quote: Clinical and demographic information for each participant, along with primary MRI findings, neurovascular compression grade (according to the Sindou classification, if suitable), and surgical procedure type are provided in the “participants.tsv” file.

required_variables.name: Sex and age (age- and sex-matched demographic fields)
required_variables.evidence_quote: Sex- and age-matched healthy controls (n = 48, ages 38–75, 55% female)... 103 patients... (ages 30–84, 56% female). Additionally, nine patients receiving conservative outpatient management... (ages 39–71, 89% female).

required_variables.name: Surgical outcome classification (favorable vs. unfavorable)
required_variables.evidence_quote: The surgical outcome was classified as favorable or unfavorable on the basis of the extent of pain reduction following the procedure.

required_variables.name: T1w/T2w structural and DTI imaging features for classifier input
required_variables.evidence_quote: the “anat” folder contains high-resolution defaced structural images (T1w and T2w)... the “dwi” folder contains diffusion tensor imaging data with 64 diffusion directions

projects.compute: Workstation-level; feature extraction via FreeSurfer/radiomics is CPU-bound, classifier training itself is lightweight (scikit-learn, CPU).
projects.still_open_because: Src e1 states that surgery type itself is chosen partly on Sindou-graded compression status (MVD for compression-positive vs. rhizotomy/balloon for compression-negative idiopathic cases), and separately reports a stark sex imbalance between the surgical (56% F) and conservatively-managed (89% F) subgroups, but nothing in the evidence indicates any stratified evaluation of a predictive model's performance across these subgroups was performed on this dataset.

projects.question_en: Can embeddings from a pretrained 3D brain-MRI foundation model, applied to this dataset's defaced T1w/T2w structural images, few-shot classify trigeminal neuralgia patients vs. matched healthy controls more accurately (and with fewer labeled examples) than a convolutional network trained from scratch on this ~160-subject cohort?
projects.question_es: ¿Los embeddings de un modelo de fundación de RM cerebral 3D preentrenado, aplicados a las imágenes estructurales T1w/T2w desfiguradas (defaced) de este dataset, clasifican en modo few-shot a pacientes con neuralgia del trigémino frente a controles sanos emparejados con mayor precisión (y con menos ejemplos etiquetados) que una red convolucional entrenada desde cero en esta cohorte de ~160 sujetos?

required_variables.name: Defaced structural T1w/T2w volumes, labeled by group via BIDS folder convention (sub-*** patients, sub-** controls)
required_variables.evidence_quote: Structural series (T1w and T2w) were defaced with Pydeface software... structural and functional MRI volumes for 112 patients with PTN, organized in folders labeled sub-***, and 48 healthy volunteers in folders labeled sub-**

required_variables.name: Clinical/demographic diagnosis and group fields for label verification
required_variables.evidence_quote: Clinical and demographic information for each participant... are provided in the “participants.tsv” file. The variables are detailed in the “participants.json” file.

skills: working with a pretrained 3D medical-imaging foundation model checkpoint
projects.compute: Single consumer GPU (8-16GB VRAM) sufficient for inference/embedding extraction and linear-probe fine-tuning on ~160 T1w/T2w volumes; full from-scratch CNN training as the comparison baseline is the more compute-intensive arm but still feasible on one GPU given the small N.
projects.still_open_because: Src e1 documents the raw BIDS-formatted, defaced structural data and group labels needed for such a comparison but reports only classical group-difference neuroimaging analyses (DTI tractometry, thalamic volumetry) from the same team's prior papers, not any deep-learning or foundation-model classification benchmark on this cohort; the small N (160) is exactly the low-label regime foundation-model few-shot transfer is meant to address.
